mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 20-day returns in > 60% of observations during tightening cycles, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with restrictive federal funds rates creates stagflationary pressure that erodes real corporate margins and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%, or the interaction effect reverses sign in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding the 2.0% threshold confirms robust aggregate demand that outpaces corporate cost structures, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% is followed by negative 20-day returns in > 58% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production alongside decelerating consumer prices signals supply-side easing that alleviates cost-push inflation risks, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 5-day returns in > 62% of triggered instances, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive effects.
